title: Buzz Access Cover Letter — Marketing Coordinator pitch
html:
TL;DR

  • Oct 7 2025 cover letter for a Marketing Coordinator role at Buzz Access LLC in Round Rock (Austin metro), 20–22 hr/week hybrid — a bridge income gig Yahir pursued ~3 weeks after landing solo in Austin.
  • Pitch leans on Brotein cofounder cred for email marketing (Klaviyo-style automation, segmentation, A/B), Facebook Ads strategy/optimization, and CRM build.
  • References Occam Advisors for ClickUp / pipeline-management chops — same Portland summer 2023 internship that anchored his pre-grad resume.
  • Names the actual stack the job advertises: HubSpot, PageFly, One Click Upsell, Slack — "I'm a quick learner, confident I can master [these] very quickly."
  • Phone (REDACTED) + personal Gmail in header; signed off as Yahir Gonzalez.

Notes

This sits in the same Oct-2025 Austin-job-search bin as 2025 10 20 quality resume and the broader bridge-income hunt that follows the alone in Austin moment. The line "the 20–22 hours per week schedule aligns perfectly with my availability" is the giveaway: Brotein and the AEO agency aren't generating cash yet, so part-time marketing work is the chosen runway pattern (vs. full-time corporate, vs. no runway starvation).

Notable: Yahir frames his CS degree as a "strong technical foundation to quickly master new marketing tools" — the same rhetorical move he uses elsewhere when applying for non-CS roles. The cover letter overstates ClickUp ("experienced user") to match the JD's "ClickUp" requirement — a quiet tell that this isn't a passion-job, it's a bridge.

Connections

  • 2025 10 20 quality resume — companion artifact in the same Austin job hunt
  • 2025 09 20 on austin arrival — the solo-arrival context
  • brotein — the credential being leveraged
  • occam advisors — the proof-point for project management
  • bridge income strategies — the operating frame

related.label: On Austin — the arrival reflection (alone, broke, ambition, Cam frustration, Hyde Park gym pitch)
